text.title: Cognitive Inclusion Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when applying cognitive-inclusion-design; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Plain-language mode applied globally; expert users complain of childlike phrasing.
antipattern.root-cause: Designer flipped the default instead of offering a toggle.
antipattern.fix: Plain language is opt-in via toggle; default targets the primary audience reading level.

antipattern.symptom: 12-step onboarding renders as one long page; users abandon mid-way.
antipattern.fix: Chunk into <=5-step pages with progress (`Step 3 of 8`); offer save-and-exit.

antipattern.symptom: Parallax hero animates on every load; OS prefers-reduced-motion ignored.
antipattern.fix: Wrap every animation in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { ... none }`; expose in-product override.
